draft.introduction: Joshua 1:1–9 records God’s commission to Joshua after Moses’s death. The repeated command “be strong and courageous” frames the conquest as dependent not on military prowess but on faithfulness to the Law. From the Septuagint.

translation.text: And it came to pass after the death of Moses the servant of the Lord, the Lord said to Joshua son of Nun, Moses’s attendant: “Moses my servant is dead. Now therefore arise and cross over the Jordan, you and all this people, into the land that I am giving to them, to the sons of Israel. Every place on which the sole of your foot shall tread, I have given it to you, just as I spoke to Moses.”
translation.original_text: καὶ ἐγένετο μετὰ τὴν τελευτὴν Μωυσῆ δούλου κυρίου

translation.text: Be strong and very courageous, being careful to do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it to the right or to the left, that you may act wisely wherever you go. This book of the law shall not depart from your mouth, and you shall meditate on it day and night. Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.

translator_notes.note: The command to meditate on the law “day and night” (ημερας και νυκτος) echoes Psalm 1:2 and became a proof text for the monastic practice of continuous Scripture meditation. The Hebrew hagah implies murmuring or reciting aloud, not silent contemplation.
